content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to actively prioritize which new experiences an agent stores in its long-term memory, solving the 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory bottleneck of unfiltered memory bloat. This creates a self-tuning memory system where an agent only retains high-uncertainty, learning-driving experiences that push its performance forward, rather than storing every trivial interaction.
